steps.title: Look at what flanks the blank
steps.content: Before the blank: "two varieties of the sweet potato" — a complete independent clause that announces a category. After the blank: "TN57, which is known for its insect resistance, and TN66, which is much more susceptible to pests" — the specific items that name and elaborate on those two varieties.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: A colon following an independent clause is the standard way to introduce a list or specification that elaborates on what just came before. That fits this sentence exactly.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'plant.': a period turns the second half into a fragment ("TN57, which is..., and TN66, which is..." has no main verb). ✗
  • Choice B — 'plant;': a semicolon joins two independent clauses, but the second half is a list of names, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'plant': no punctuation leaves the list of varieties dangling without the needed introduction. ✗
  • Choice D — 'plant:': a colon correctly introduces the elaborating list of the two specific varieties. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
